title: Peppery Indian Sauerkraut Curry
description: A cozy, low-carb Indian dish featuring tender sauerkraut in a peppery curry sauce. This recipe is perfect for a chilly evening.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it's translucent and starting to brown.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the drained sauerkraut to the saucepan and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Pour in 1/4 cup of water and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it cook, covered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the coconut cream and continue to cook for another 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
